Pass-through wrappers in incident-alert-handler.ts and market-alert-handler.ts are NOT pure pass-throughs — they configure severity mappings and buildParams callbacks, providing meaningful behavioral specialization via createSeverityHandler

No evidence of entry/exit debug logging patterns — logging is used purposefully at meaningful points

ai_generated_debt.dimension_character: The codebase shows moderate AI-generated comment inflation concentrated in barrel files and small utilities, where docstrings restate obvious structure. However, the core business logic files have appropriate comment-to-code ratios, and error handling patterns are properly extracted rather than duplicated. The pass-through wrappers flagged mechanically are actually meaningful factory configurations.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: Score of 82.5 reflects a codebase with minor docstring bloat in peripheral files but no systemic AI-debt patterns. The ~20 files with high comment ratios are mostly barrel exports and tiny utilities where the overhead is cosmetic rather than maintenance-burdening. No debug logging spam, no defensive overengineering, and error handling is DRY via shared helpers. Anchored between 80 (minor scattered issues) and 85 (clean).

issues.summary: Barrel/index files have docstring blocks that restate the directory name without adding insight
evidence: src/lib/auth/index.ts: '/** Auth subfolder — authentication and identity management. */' followed by 2 re-export lines — the docstring adds zero information beyond what the path already says
5 barrel files have 36-60% comment ratios for files that are purely re-export
issues.suggestion: Remove module-level docstrings from barrel files that only re-export. The file path and export list are self-documenting.

issues.summary: observe-plugin.ts explains the same concept three times in 24 lines
evidence: Line 4: 'it ensures the observe capability is discoverable alongside other plugins even though its invocation pattern differs'
Line 21: '// Observe is inline (appendFileSync), not hook-driven.'
Line 22: '// This plugin exists for registry completeness and documentation.'
All three say the same thing: observe is inline, plugin is for discoverability
issues.suggestion: Keep one comment (the module docstring) and remove the inline repetitions on lines 21-22.

issues.summary: Scoring constants have docstrings that echo the constant name without adding domain insight
evidence: SCORE_BASE = 20 has comment '/** Every post gets base points */' — adds nothing beyond the name
SCORE_ATTESTATION = 40 has '/** DAHR attestation (sourceAttestations) present — TLSN proofs do NOT score */' — this one IS useful because it clarifies TLSN exclusion
SCORE_CONFIDENCE = 5 has '/** Confidence field set on post */' — restates the name
issues.suggestion: Keep comments that add non-obvious domain rules (like the TLSN exclusion). Remove comments that restate the constant name (SCORE_BASE, SCORE_CONFIDENCE, SCORE_LONG_TEXT).

root_causes: AI-generated docstrings applied uniformly regardless of whether the code is self-documenting
likely_symptoms: barrel_docstring_bloat and observe_plugin_triple_restate share the root cause of AI over-documenting

add.header: Error handling is DRY via shared helpers
add.description: Toolkit tools use demosError() and tool-wrapper.ts for consistent error handling rather than copy-pasting try-catch boilerplate. This is proper extraction, not AI-generated duplication.

add.header: No debug logging spam patterns detected
